# @tac-ui/web Detailed Documentation

`@tac-ui/web` is a premium, cross-platform design system providing 47+ React components. This file serves as an extremely detailed usage and props specification for LLMs and developers.

## Quick Start & Theming

```bash
pnpm add @tac-ui/web @tac-ui/icon @tac-ui/tokens
```

Wrap your application in `TacProvider` to enable theming (Dark/Light mode) and context configuration:

```tsx
import { TacProvider } from '@tac-ui/web';

export default function App({ children }) {
  // You can optionally pass defaultMode="dark" or "light"
  return <TacProvider>{children}</TacProvider>;
}
```

### Tailwind Configuration
In your `tailwind.config.ts`, include the preset:
```ts
import { tacPreset } from '@tac-ui/web/tailwind';

export default {
  presets: [tacPreset],
  content: [
    // Ensure you scan the package for tailwind classes
    './node_modules/@tac-ui/web/dist/**/*.js',
    './src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'
  ]
};
```

---

## Components Detail Reference

### 1. Button

An interactive element used to trigger actions. It supports various visual variants, sizes, and icon placements.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@tac-ui/web';
import { Search } from '@tac-ui/icon';

// Basic
<Button variant="primary">Submit</Button>

// With Icons
<Button leftIcon={<Search size={16} />} variant="outline">Search</Button>

// Icon Only
<Button iconOnly aria-label="Search"><Search size={16} /></Button>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'primary' \| 'secondary' \| 'outline' \| 'ghost' \| 'point' \| 'destructive'` | `'primary'` | Defines the visual hierarchy and style of the button. |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | The size of the button, affecting padding and font size. |
| `iconOnly` | `boolean` | `false` | If true, adjusts padding to be perfectly square for icon-only usage. |
| `leftIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Icon element to render on the left side of the text. |
| `rightIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Icon element to render on the right side of the text. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables interaction and dims the button visually. |
| `onClick` | `(e: React.MouseEvent) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when the button is pressed. |
| `type` | `'button' \| 'submit' \| 'reset'` | `'button'` | Standard HTML button type. |

---

### 2. Input

A standard text entry field used to gather user input. Supports labels, helper text, and error states.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Input } from '@tac-ui/web';
import { Mail } from '@tac-ui/icon';

// Basic with Label
<Input label="Email Address" placeholder="name@example.com" />

// With Error State
<Input 
  label="Username" 
  error={true} 
  errorMessage="Username is already taken." 
  leftIcon={<Mail size={16} />}
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `inputSize` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Specifies the height and padding of the input field. |
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered above the input field. |
| `helperText` | `string` | `undefined` | Contextual text rendered below the input field. |
| `error` | `boolean` | `false` | Toggles the error visual styling (usually red borders). |
| `errorMessage` | `string` | `undefined` | Text displayed below the input when `error` is true. Overrides `helperText`. |
| `leftIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Icon rendered inside the input on the left side. |
| `rightIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Icon rendered inside the input on the right side. |
| `rightButton` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Interactive element (like a clear button or password toggle) on the right side. |
| `...props` | `InputHTMLAttributes` | - | Accepts all standard HTML input attributes (value, onChange, placeholder, disabled, etc). |

---

### 3. Textarea

A multi-line plain-text editing control.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Textarea } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Textarea 
  label="Biography" 
  placeholder="Tell us about yourself..." 
  rows={6}
  helperText="Maximum 500 characters."
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `textareaSize` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Specifies the base font size and padding. |
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered above the textarea. |
| `helperText` | `string` | `undefined` | Contextual text rendered below the textarea. |
| `error` | `boolean` | `false` | Toggles error visual styling. |
| `errorMessage` | `string` | `undefined` | Text displayed below when `error` is true. |
| `rows` | `number` | `3` | Number of visible text lines. |
| `...props` | `TextareaHTMLAttributes` | - | Accepts standard HTML textarea attributes. |

---

### 4. Select

A dropdown interface for choosing a value from a predefined list.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Select } from '@tac-ui/web';
import { useState } from 'react';

const [country, setCountry] = useState('');

<Select
  label="Country"
  placeholder="Select your country..."
  value={country}
  onChange={setCountry}
  options={[
    { value: 'us', label: 'United States' },
    { value: 'kr', label: 'South Korea' },
    { value: 'jp', label: 'Japan', disabled: true },
  ]}
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered above the select. |
| `options` | `Array<{ value: string; label: string; disabled?: boolean }>` | `[]` | The list of available options to display in the dropdown. |
| `value` | `string` | `undefined` | The currently selected value. |
| `onChange` | `(value: string) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when an option is selected. |
| `placeholder` | `string` | `'Select...'` | Initial text shown when no value is selected. |
| `selectSize` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | The size of the trigger button. |
| `error` | `boolean` | `false` | Toggles error visual styling. |
| `errorMessage` | `string` | `undefined` | Text displayed below when `error` is true. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables the entire select interaction. |

---

### 5. Combobox

An input widget with a dropdown list of options, typically used when options need to be searchable.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Combobox } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Combobox
  placeholder="Search for a framework..."
  options={[
    { value: 'react', label: 'React' },
    { value: 'vue', label: 'Vue' },
    { value: 'angular', label: 'Angular' }
  ]}
  value={selected}
  onChange={setSelected}
  emptyText="No frameworks found."
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `options` | `Array<{ value: string; label: string; disabled?: boolean }>` | `[]` | The list of available searchable options. |
| `value` | `string` | `undefined` | The currently selected value. |
| `onChange` | `(value: string) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when an option is selected. |
| `placeholder` | `string` | `undefined` | Placeholder text for the search input. |
| `emptyText` | `string` | `'No results found'` | Text displayed when the search yields no matching options. |

---

### 6. Card

A container used to encapsulate related information and actions. Often composed with sub-components.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter, Button } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Card variant="accent" interactive>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Premium Subscription</CardTitle>
    <CardDescription>Upgrade to unlock advanced features.</CardDescription>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    <p>Access unlimited projects and priority support.</p>
  </CardContent>
  <CardFooter>
    <Button variant="point">Upgrade Now</Button>
  </CardFooter>
</Card>
```

**Props (Card Root):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'accent' \| 'glass' \| 'flat'` | `'default'` | Determines the background and border styling of the card. |
| `interactive` | `boolean` | `false` | If true, adds hover transformations and focuses styling to make the card act like a button. |
| `tilt` | `boolean` | `false` | If true, adds a 3D tilt effect on hover (often requires Framer Motion). |

**Sub-components:**
- **`CardHeader`**: Wraps `CardTitle` and `CardDescription` with appropriate spacing.
- **`CardTitle`**: Renders an `h3` styling for the card title.
- **`CardDescription`**: Renders muted text for subtitles.
- **`CardContent`**: The main body wrapper with padding.
- **`CardFooter`**: A wrapper for actions at the bottom of the card, usually flexbox layout.

---

### 7. Badge

A small component typically used to display a status, label, or count.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Badge } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Badge variant="success">Completed</Badge>
<Badge variant="destructive" interactive>Delete</Badge>
<Badge count={12} /> // Renders a small numerical dot
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'secondary' \| 'destructive' \| 'outline' \| 'success' \| 'error' \| 'warning' \| 'info' \| 'glass'` | `'default'` | The color thematic variant of the badge. |
| `interactive` | `boolean` | `false` | If true, applies hover styling. |
| `count` | `number` | `undefined` | If provided, styles the badge as a numerical counter circle. |

---

### 8. Checkbox

A control that allows the user to toggle a true/false or selected/unselected state.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Checkbox } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Checkbox 
  label="I agree to the Terms of Service" 
  checked={isChecked} 
  onChange={(e) => setIsChecked(e.target.checked)} 
/>

// Indeterminate state (useful for "Select All" functionality)
<Checkbox label="Select All" indeterminate={isIndeterminate} />
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered next to the checkbox input. |
| `checked` | `boolean` | `undefined` | Controlled value representing the checked state. |
| `defaultChecked` | `boolean` | `false` | Uncontrolled initial checked state. |
| `indeterminate` | `boolean` | `false` | Displays a dash icon instead of a checkmark, overriding `checked` visually. |
| `filled` | `boolean` | `false` | Uses a filled background variant instead of a border outline when unchecked. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables interaction and dims opacity. |
| `onChange` | `(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void` | `undefined` | Native change event handler. |

---

### 9. Radio & RadioGroup

Mutually exclusive option selection.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { RadioGroup, Radio } from '@tac-ui/web';

<RadioGroup value={plan} onValueChange={setPlan}>
  <Radio radioValue="basic" label="Basic Plan - $9/mo" />
  <Radio radioValue="pro" label="Pro Plan - $29/mo" />
  <Radio radioValue="enterprise" label="Enterprise" disabled />
</RadioGroup>
```

**Props (RadioGroup):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `string` | `undefined` | Controlled value matching a child Radio `radioValue`. |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | `undefined` | Uncontrolled initial value. |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when a child Radio is selected. |

**Props (Radio):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `radioValue` | `string` | *Required* | The string value this radio button represents. |
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered next to the radio circle. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables interaction for this specific option. |

---

### 10. Switch

A physical toggle switch used for boolean preferences.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Switch } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Switch 
  label="Enable Notifications" 
  checked={notificationsEnabled} 
  onChange={setNotificationsEnabled} 
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered next to the switch track. |
| `checked` | `boolean` | `undefined` | Controlled checked state. |
| `defaultChecked` | `boolean` | `false` | Uncontrolled initial checked state. |
| `onChange` | `(checked: boolean) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when toggled, providing the new boolean state. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables interaction. |

---

### 11. Slider

An input where the user selects a value from within a given range by sliding a thumb.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Slider } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Slider 
  label="Brightness" 
  min={0} 
  max={100} 
  step={5}
  value={brightness} 
  onChange={(e) => setBrightness(Number(e.target.value))} 
  showValue
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered above the slider. |
| `showValue` | `boolean` | `false` | If true, renders the current numerical value next to the label. |
| `filled` | `boolean` | `true` | Determines if the track before the thumb should be filled with color. |
| `min` | `number` | `0` | The minimum allowed value. |
| `max` | `number` | `100` | The maximum allowed value. |
| `step` | `number` | `1` | The increment interval. |
| `value` | `number` | `undefined` | Controlled value. |
| `defaultValue` | `number` | `undefined` | Uncontrolled initial value. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables interaction. |

---

### 12. SegmentController

A linear set of two or more segments, each of which functions as a toggle. Often used as an alternative to Tabs or a Select dropdown for a small number of options.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { SegmentController } from '@tac-ui/web';

<SegmentController
  value={view}
  onChange={setView}
  options={[
    { value: 'list', label: 'List View', icon: <List /> },
    { value: 'grid', label: 'Grid View', icon: <Grid /> }
  ]}
  fullWidth
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `options` | `Array<{ value: string; label: string; icon?: ReactNode; disabled?: boolean }>` | *Required* | The segments to render. |
| `mode` | `'single' \| 'multi'` | `'single'` | Determines if multiple segments can be active simultaneously. |
| `value` | `string \| string[]` | `undefined` | Controlled value (`string[]` if `mode="multi"`). |
| `defaultValue` | `string \| string[]` | `undefined` | Uncontrolled initial value. |
| `onChange` | `(value: string \| string[]) => void` | `undefined` | Event handler when a segment is tapped. |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Scale of the controller. |
| `fullWidth` | `boolean` | `false` | If true, expands to fill the parent container, distributing segments evenly. |
| `collapsible` | `boolean` | `false` | For use in responsive layouts (collapses options into a dropdown conditionally). |

---

### 13. DatePicker

A fully featured calendar and date selection interface.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { DatePicker } from '@tac-ui/web';

<DatePicker 
  label="Meeting Date" 
  mode="datetime" 
  value={date} 
  onChange={setDate} 
  use24Hour={true}
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `Date \| null` | `null` | The currently selected date object. |
| `onChange` | `(date: Date \| null) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when the user selects a date/time. |
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Text rendered above the input trigger. |
| `placeholder` | `string` | `'Select date'` | Text in the input trigger when value is null. |
| `mode` | `'date' \| 'datetime' \| 'month'` | `'date'` | The selection mode. `datetime` shows time pickers; `month` only allows picking a year/month. |
| `use24Hour` | `boolean` | `true` | Formats time options in 24-hour style (e.g. 14:00 vs 2:00 PM). |
| `minuteStep` | `number` | `1` | Interval for the minutes dropdown in datetime mode. |
| `minDate` | `Date` | `undefined` | Prevents selection of dates before this date. |
| `maxDate` | `Date` | `undefined` | Prevents selection of dates after this date. |

---

### 14. ColorPicker

An interactive UI for selecting colors.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { ColorPicker } from '@tac-ui/web';

<ColorPicker 
  label="Theme Primary Color" 
  value={hexColor} 
  onChange={setHexColor} 
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `string` | `undefined` | The currently selected color in hex format (e.g., `'#ff0000'`). |
| `onChange` | `(color: string) => void` | `undefined` | Callback fired when the color changes. |
| `label` | `string` | `undefined` | Label text describing the picker. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables the component. |

---

### 15. Tabs

A set of layered sections of content, displaying one panel at a time.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Tabs, TabsList, TabTrigger, TabContent } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Tabs defaultValue="account" variant="pill">
  <TabsList>
    <TabTrigger value="account">Account</TabTrigger>
    <TabTrigger value="billing">Billing</TabTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  
  <TabContent value="account">
    <p>Update your account details here.</p>
  </TabContent>
  
  <TabContent value="billing">
    <p>Manage payment methods.</p>
  </TabContent>
</Tabs>
```

**Props (Tabs):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `string` | `undefined` | Controlled value representing the active `TabContent`. |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | `''` | Initial uncontrolled active tab. |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | `undefined` | Fired when a different `TabTrigger` is clicked. |
| `variant` | `'underline' \| 'pill' \| 'outline' \| 'icon'` | `'underline'` | Visual styling mechanism for the active trigger indicators. |

---

### 16. Accordion

A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that each reveal a section of content.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Accordion type="single" outline>
  <AccordionItem value="faq-1">
    <AccordionTrigger>What is Tac UI?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Tac UI is a premium UI component library.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="faq-2">
    <AccordionTrigger>Is it accessible?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Yes, it follows WAI-ARIA guidelines.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

**Props (Accordion Root):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `type` | `'single' \| 'multiple'` | `'single'` | Defines if only one item can open at a time (`single`) or many (`multiple`). |
| `defaultValue` | `string \| string[]` | `undefined` | Initial open items. String array for `multiple` type. |
| `outline` | `boolean` | `true` | Renders borders around the accordion items. |
| `glass` | `boolean` | `false` | Uses a glassmorphism blurred background for items. |

---

### 17. Dialog

A window overlaid on either the primary window or another dialog window. Often used for confirmation prompts.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Dialog, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, Button } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Dialog open={isOpen} onClose={() => setIsOpen(false)}>
  <DialogHeader>
    <DialogTitle>Delete Project?</DialogTitle>
    <DialogDescription>This action cannot be undone.</DialogDescription>
  </DialogHeader>
  <DialogFooter>
    <Button variant="ghost" onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)}>Cancel</Button>
    <Button variant="destructive" onClick={performDelete}>Delete</Button>
  </DialogFooter>
</Dialog>
```

**Props (Dialog Root):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `open` | `boolean` | *Required* | Controlled boolean determining if the dialog is visible. |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | *Required* | Callback fired to close the dialog (e.g. clicking backdrop or pressing ESC). |
| `backdrop` | `boolean` | `true` | Renders a darkened semi-transparent backdrop behind the dialog. |
| `layoutId` | `string` | `undefined` | Used with Framer Motion for shared layout animation transitions. |

---

### 18. Modal

Similar to `Dialog` but generally used for larger, more complex interactions (like forms or large views) rather than simple confirmations.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Modal, ModalHeader, ModalTitle, ModalFooter, Input, Button } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Modal open={isOpen} onClose={() => setIsOpen(false)} size="lg">
  <ModalHeader>
    <ModalTitle>Create New Organization</ModalTitle>
  </ModalHeader>
  {/* Modal body content is placed directly as children */}
  <div className="py-4">
    <Input label="Organization Name" />
  </div>
  <ModalFooter>
    <Button variant="primary">Create</Button>
  </ModalFooter>
</Modal>
```

**Props (Modal Root):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `open` | `boolean` | *Required* | Visibility state. |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | *Required* | Handler for close requests. |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg' \| 'xl'` | `'md'` | Controls the `max-width` of the modal container. |
| `backdrop` | `boolean` | `true` | Toggles the overlay backdrop. |

---

### 19. Drawer

A panel that slides in from the edge of the screen, useful for side navigation or responsive bottom sheets.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Drawer, DrawerHeader, DrawerTitle, DrawerBody } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Drawer open={isOpen} onClose={() => setIsOpen(false)} side="right">
  <DrawerHeader>
    <DrawerTitle>Settings</DrawerTitle>
  </DrawerHeader>
  <DrawerBody>
    <p>Configuration options go here.</p>
  </DrawerBody>
</Drawer>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `open` | `boolean` | *Required* | Visibility state. |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | *Required* | Handler for close requests. |
| `side` | `'left' \| 'right' \| 'top' \| 'bottom'` | `'right'` | Which edge of the screen the drawer slides from. |
| `backdrop` | `boolean` | `true` | Toggles the overlay backdrop. |

---

### 20. Tooltip

A popup that displays information related to an element when hovered or focused.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Tooltip, Button } from '@tac-ui/web';
import { HelpCircle } from '@tac-ui/icon';

<Tooltip content="Provides extra context about this feature" placement="top">
  <Button variant="ghost" iconOnly><HelpCircle size={16} /></Button>
</Tooltip>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `content` | `React.ReactNode` | *Required* | The primary text or element shown inside the tooltip bubble. |
| `description` | `string` | `undefined` | Optional secondary muted text under the main content. |
| `placement` | `'top' \| 'bottom' \| 'left' \| 'right'` | `'top'` | Preferred position relative to the trigger element. |
| `delay` | `number` | `200` | Delay in milliseconds before showing the tooltip on hover. |

---

### 21. Popover

Floating content attached to an element, displayed on click. Unlike a tooltip, it usually contains interactive elements like forms or filters.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Popover, PopoverHeader, PopoverBody, Button, Input } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Popover trigger={<Button>Filter Options</Button>} side="bottom" align="start">
  <PopoverHeader>Quick Filter</PopoverHeader>
  <PopoverBody>
    <Input placeholder="Search criteria..." />
  </PopoverBody>
</Popover>
```

**Props (Popover Root):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `trigger` | `React.ReactNode` | *Required* | The element the user clicks to open the popover. |
| `open` | `boolean` | `undefined` | Optional controlled state. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | `undefined` | Callback for state changes. |
| `side` | `'top' \| 'bottom' \| 'left' \| 'right'` | `'bottom'` | Position relative to the trigger. |
| `align` | `'start' \| 'center' \| 'end'` | `'center'` | Alignment along the orthogonal axis. |

---

### 22. Dropdown

Displays a list of action options upon interaction.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Dropdown, DropdownItem, DropdownDivider, Button } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Dropdown trigger={<Button>Actions</Button>} align="end">
  <DropdownItem onClick={() => edit()}>Edit</DropdownItem>
  <DropdownItem onClick={() => duplicate()}>Duplicate</DropdownItem>
  <DropdownDivider />
  <DropdownItem onClick={() => destroy()} destructive>Delete</DropdownItem>
</Dropdown>
```

**Props (Dropdown Root):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `trigger` | `React.ReactNode` | *Required* | Element that opens the dropdown. |
| `align` | `'start' \| 'center' \| 'end'` | `'start'` | Alignment of the dropdown menu relative to the trigger. |
| `open` / `onOpenChange` | `boolean` / `function` | - | Controlled state options. |

---

### 23. Toast

A succinct, non-blocking notification. Requires setting up the Context provider.

**Setup & Usage:**
```tsx
// 1. In your App root
import { ToastProvider, useToast, Button } from '@tac-ui/web';

export default function App() {
  return (
    <ToastProvider position="bottom-right" maxToasts={3}>
      <ExamplePage />
    </ToastProvider>
  );
}

// 2. In your component
function ExamplePage() {
  const { toast } = useToast();

  return (
    <Button onClick={() => {
      toast('Operation successful!', { 
        variant: 'success', 
        duration: 3000 
      });
    }}>
      Save
    </Button>
  );
}
```

**Toast Options (second argument to `toast()`):**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'success' \| 'error' \| 'warning' \| 'info'` | `'default'` | Visual coloring. |
| `duration` | `number` | `5000` | Time in ms before auto-dismissal. `0` means never auto-dismiss. |
| `action` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Custom React element, usually a button like "Undo". |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Custom icon override. |

---

### 24. Table

A structured grid to display data rows and columns natively formatted.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Table>
  <TableHeader>
    <TableRow>
      <TableHead>User ID</TableHead>
      <TableHead>Name</TableHead>
      <TableHead>Role</TableHead>
    </TableRow>
  </TableHeader>
  <TableBody>
    <TableRow>
      <TableCell>1</TableCell>
      <TableCell>Alice</TableCell>
      <TableCell>Admin</TableCell>
    </TableRow>
  </TableBody>
</Table>
```

---

### 25. Pagination

Controls for navigating between pages of content.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Pagination, PaginationContent, PaginationItem, PaginationPrevious, PaginationNext, PaginationEllipsis } from '@tac-ui/web';

<Pagination>
  <PaginationContent>
    <PaginationPrevious onClick={() => setPage(p => p - 1)} />
    <PaginationItem isActive>1</PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem onClick={() => setPage(2)}>2</PaginationItem>
    <PaginationEllipsis />
    <PaginationItem onClick={() => setPage(10)}>10</PaginationItem>
    <PaginationNext onClick={() => setPage(p => p + 1)} />
  </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>
```
*Note: `PaginationItem` takes an `isActive` boolean prop to highlight the current page.*

---

### 26. Label

An accessible form label with optional required indicator, optional suffix, and tooltip support.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Label } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Basic
<Label htmlFor="email">Email address</Label>

// Required field
<Label htmlFor="password" required>Password</Label>

// Optional field
<Label htmlFor="bio" showOptional>Bio</Label>

// With tooltip
<Label htmlFor="api-key" required tooltip="Your secret API key from the dashboard.">
  API Key
</Label>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `required` | `boolean` | `false` | When true, appends a red asterisk to indicate the field is required. |
| `showOptional` | `boolean` | `false` | When true, appends a grey "(optional)" suffix. |
| `tooltip` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Tooltip content shown next to the label via an info icon. |
| `htmlFor` | `string` | `undefined` | Associates the label with a form control by its id. |
| `...props` | `LabelHTMLAttributes` | - | Accepts all standard HTML label attributes. |

---

### 27. Banner

A full-width page-level notification bar, typically placed at the top of a page or section. Use Banner for page-level alerts; use Alert for inline content-level feedback.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Banner } from '@tac-ui/web';
import { Info, AlertTriangle } from '@tac-ui/icon';

// Basic
<Banner title="New feature available" description="Try the updated dashboard." />

// With icon and variant
<Banner variant="warning" icon={<AlertTriangle />} title="Trial ending soon" description="Upgrade to keep access." />

// Dismissible
<Banner variant="error" title="Payment failed" dismissible onDismiss={() => console.log('dismissed')} />
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'warning' \| 'error' \| 'success'` | `'default'` | Visual style variant. Default uses info color tokens. |
| `title` | `string` | `undefined` | Optional title displayed in bold. |
| `description` | `string` | `undefined` | Optional description text below the title. |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Optional icon displayed to the left. Icon color matches the variant. |
| `dismissible` | `boolean` | `false` | When true, shows a close button that animates the banner out. |
| `onDismiss` | `() => void` | `undefined` | Called when the dismiss button is clicked. |

---

### 28. Link

A styled anchor element with variant styles and optional external link support.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Link } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Inline (default) — colored underlined link for body text
<p>Read the <Link href="/docs">full documentation</Link> for details.</p>

// Plain — no underline, for navigation
<Link href="/settings" variant="plain">Go to settings</Link>

// Current — marks the active page, non-interactive
<Link href="/about" variant="current">About</Link>

// External — opens in new tab with icon
<Link href="https://example.com" external>Visit Example</Link>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'inline' \| 'plain' \| 'current'` | `'inline'` | Visual style variant. |
| `external` | `boolean` | `false` | When true, opens in a new tab and appends an external link icon. |
| `...props` | `AnchorHTMLAttributes` | - | Accepts all standard HTML anchor attributes (href, target, etc). |

---

### 29. Grid & GridItem

Responsive grid layout with predefined column presets. Provides 9 layout variants that adapt across breakpoints.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Grid, GridItem } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Three equal columns
<Grid variant="3up" gap="md">
  <GridItem>Column 1</GridItem>
  <GridItem>Column 2</GridItem>
  <GridItem>Column 3</GridItem>
</Grid>

// Wide-left layout (2:1 ratio)
<Grid variant="2-1" gap="lg">
  <GridItem>Main content</GridItem>
  <GridItem>Sidebar</GridItem>
</Grid>

// GridItem with explicit span
<Grid variant="4up">
  <GridItem span={2}>Wide item</GridItem>
  <GridItem>Item</GridItem>
  <GridItem>Item</GridItem>
</Grid>
```

**Grid Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'2up' \| 'side-by-side' \| '2-1' \| '1-2' \| '1-3up' \| '3up' \| '4up' \| '6up' \| '1-2-4up'` | `'2up'` | Layout preset that defines the column structure. |
| `gap` | `'none' \| 'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Gap size between grid cells. |

**GridItem Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `span` | `number` | `undefined` | Number of columns this item should span. |

---

### 30. Collapsible

A single disclosure panel with animated expand/collapse. Supports both controlled and uncontrolled modes. Use Collapsible for a single panel; use Accordion for multiple related sections.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Collapsible } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Uncontrolled
<Collapsible label="Show details">
  Hidden content revealed on click.
</Collapsible>

// Default open
<Collapsible label="FAQ" defaultOpen>
  This content is visible by default.
</Collapsible>

// Controlled
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
<Collapsible label="Settings" open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  Controlled panel content.
</Collapsible>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `React.ReactNode` | *Required* | The label displayed in the trigger button. |
| `open` | `boolean` | `undefined` | Controlled open state. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | `undefined` | Called when the open state changes. |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` | `false` | Initial open state for uncontrolled mode. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | *Required* | Content revealed when the panel is open. |

---

### 31. Meter

Displays a scalar measurement within a known range. The bar color changes based on fill level: green under 60%, yellow 60-80%, red above 80%. Use Meter for static values (storage, scores); use Progress for ongoing operations (uploads, loading).

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { Meter } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Basic percentage
<Meter value={42} label="Storage used" />

// Custom value display
<Meter value={72} label="Storage" customValue="7.2 GB / 10 GB" />

// Custom range
<Meter value={3} min={0} max={5} label="Rating" customValue="3 / 5 stars" />

// Hidden value
<Meter value={60} label="Progress" showValue={false} />
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `number` | *Required* | Current meter value. |
| `min` | `number` | `0` | Minimum value of the range. |
| `max` | `number` | `100` | Maximum value of the range. |
| `label` | `string` | *Required* | Label displayed to the left of the value. |
| `customValue` | `string` | `undefined` | Custom display text. When omitted, shows percentage. |
| `showValue` | `boolean` | `true` | When true, displays the value to the right of the label. |

---

### 32. ClipboardText

Displays a monospace text value with a one-click copy-to-clipboard button that appears on hover.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { ClipboardText } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Basic
<ClipboardText text="npm install @tac-ui/web" />

// Small size
<ClipboardText text="sk-proj-abc123" size="sm" />

// With callback
<ClipboardText text="https://api.example.com" onCopy={() => console.log('copied!')} />
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `text` | `string` | *Required* | The text value to display and copy to clipboard. |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Controls padding, font size, and icon size. |
| `onCopy` | `() => void` | `undefined` | Called after the text is successfully copied. |

---

### 33. SensitiveInput

A secure input that masks its value by default and reveals on explicit user action. Has three visual states: masked (shows bullets with hover-to-copy), revealed (editable text input), and empty (standard input). Keyboard accessible: Enter/Space to reveal, Escape to re-mask.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import { SensitiveInput, Label } from '@tac-ui/web';

// Basic controlled
const [apiKey, setApiKey] = useState('sk-proj-abc123...');
<SensitiveInput value={apiKey} onChange={(e) => setApiKey(e.target.value)} />

// With Label
<div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
  <Label htmlFor="secret" required>API Key</Label>
  <SensitiveInput
    id="secret"
    value={apiKey}
    onChange={(e) => setApiKey(e.target.value)}
    placeholder="Enter your API key"
  />
</div>

// With callbacks
<SensitiveInput
  value={token}
  onChange={(e) => setToken(e.target.value)}
  onCopy={() => console.log('copied without revealing')}
  onReveal={() => console.log('value revealed')}
/>
```

**Props:**
| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `string` | `undefined` | Controlled value. When non-empty, starts in masked state. |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | `undefined` | Uncontrolled default value. |
| `onCopy` | `() => void` | `undefined` | Called after the value is copied from the masked state. |
| `onReveal` | `() => void` | `undefined` | Called when transitioning from masked to revealed state. |
| `...props` | `InputHTMLAttributes` | - | Accepts all standard HTML input attributes except `type` (managed internally). |

---

### Additional Primitives

- **`Avatar`**: Prop `src` (string), `initials` (string), `size` ('sm'|'md'|'lg'|'xl'), `showStatus` (boolean). Displays user images or fallback initials.
- **`Chip`**: Prop `variant` ('filter'|'input'|'glass'), `onDelete` (function). Small text labels.
- **`Progress`**: Prop `value` (number 0-100), `variant` ('linear'|'circular'). Shows loading completion.
- **`Skeleton`**: Prop `variant` ('text'|'circular'|'rectangular'), `animation` ('shimmer'|'pulse'). Loading placeholders.
- **`Alert`**: Prop `variant` ('success'|'error'|'info'|'warning'), `dismissible` (boolean). Static inline messages.
- **`CodeBlock`**: Prop `code` (string), `language` (string), `showLineNumbers` (boolean). Syntax highlighted code snippet.
- **`EmptyState`**: Prop `icon` (node), `title` (string), `description` (string), `action` (node). Illustration for empty tables/pages.
- **`Stepper`**: Prop `activeStep` (number 0-indexed), `orientation` ('horizontal'|'vertical'). Shows workflow progress through `<Step title="..." />` children.
- **`Divider`**: Visual HR line. Prop `label` allows rendering text in the center like "OR".
- **`MorphingCard`**: Advanced Framer Motion component. Prop `layoutId` (required), renders a `preview` prop and morphs into a full `detail` prop node on click.

*(All styling leverages the underlying Tailwind utility sets configured by the `tacPreset` in the tailwind config and the design tokens injected by `TacProvider`).*
